title: New gh actions CI system
body:
see commits

Note I did not yet drop all the old cirrus stuff to try to keep the size more manageable and some of the cirrus script are worth saving, i.e. the logformatter.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Additionally, as a maintainer, I currently don't have the permissions to re-run failed jobs. I believe we should enable this feature, though it's not a high priority right now.

> Additionally, as a maintainer, I currently don't have the permissions to re-run failed jobs. I believe we should enable this feature, though it's not a high priority right now.

You can, the problem is github actions. You can only re-run all jobs once all jobs finished, do not re-run individual jobs and also only re-run failed jobs, I show it later in the meeting.

repushed with a the fix for the broken "Total Success" status on the windows task, the problem was the leftover continue-on-error on the windows machine task which sets the status always to success.

I hope it works properly now, likely need to wait for quay.io to get better first.
